query:
You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.61 (commit: 44a38b08).

## Issue to Resolve

🤖 Cancel stale requests on domain search screen
When searching for a new domain (to purchase) within the domain management flow, the results of multiple requests are loaded sequentially after typing. Only the latest request should be used to update the pending / loading screen.

Note that this is a separate concern from the debounce delay (used to throttle requests and reduce wasted fetches on keystrokes). Increasing the debounce delay _could_ mitigate the buggy results ux, but this has a tradeoff of increasing the total time to success. Instead, we should ensure that only the response to the latest request can update the ui state.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/domains/management/newdomainsearch/NewDomainSearchViewModel.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.management.newdomainsearch
  2→
  3→import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
  4→import dagger.hilt.android.lifecycle.HiltViewModel
  5→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineDispatcher
  6→import kotlinx.coroutines.FlowPreview
  7→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
  8→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableSharedFlow
  9→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
 10→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
 11→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.debounce
 12→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.filter
 13→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.launchIn
 14→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.onEach
 15→import org.wordpress.android.analytics.AnalyticsTracker
 16→import org.wordpress.android.modules.UI_THREAD
 17→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.management.newdomainsearch.domainsfetcher.NewDomainsSearchRepository
 18→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.management.newdomainsearch.domainsfetcher.ProposedDomain
 19→import org.wordpress.android.util.analytics.AnalyticsTrackerWrapper
 20→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ScopedViewModel
 21→import javax.inject.Inject
 22→import javax.inject.Named
 23→
 24→private const val SEARCH_QUERY_DELAY_MS = 250L
 25→
 26→@OptIn(FlowPreview::class)
 27→@HiltViewModel
 28→class NewDomainSearchViewModel @Inject constructor(
 29→    @Named(UI_THREAD) private val mainDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 30→    private val newDomainsSearchRepository: NewDomainsSearchRepository,
 31→    private val analyticsTracker: AnalyticsTrackerWrapper
 32→) : ScopedViewModel(mainDispatcher) {
 33→    private val _actionEvents = MutableSharedFlow<ActionEvent>()
 34→    val actionEvents: Flow<ActionEvent> = _actionEvents
 35→
 36→    private val _uiStateFlow: MutableStateFlow<UiState> = MutableStateFlow(UiState.PopulatedDomains(emptyList()))
 37→    val uiStateFlow = _uiStateFlow.asStateFlow()
 38→
 39→    private val debouncedQuery = MutableStateFlow("")
 40→
 41→    init {
 42→        analyticsTracker.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.DOMAIN_MANAGEMENT_SEARCH_FOR_A_DOMAIN_SCREEN_SHOWN)
 43→        debouncedQuery
 44→            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
 45→            .debounce(SEARCH_QUERY_DELAY_MS)
 46→            .onEach(::fetchDomains)
 47→            .launchIn(viewModelScope)
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    private suspend fun fetchDomains(query: String) {
 51→        _uiStateFlow.emit(UiState.Loading)
 52→        val result = newDomainsSearchRepository.searchForDomains(query)
 53→        _uiStateFlow.emit(
 54→            when (result) {
 55→                is NewDomainsSearchRepository.DomainsResult.Success -> UiState.PopulatedDomains(result.proposedDomains)
 56→                is NewDomainsSearchRepository.DomainsResult.Error -> UiState.Error
 57→            }
 58→        )
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    fun onSearchQueryChanged(query: String) {
 62→        launch {
 63→           debouncedQuery.emit(query.trim())
 64→        }
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    fun onTransferDomainClicked() {
 68→        analyticsTracker.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.DOMAIN_MANAGEMENT_TRANSFER_DOMAIN_TAPPED)
 69→        launch {
 70→            _actionEvents.emit(ActionEvent.TransferDomain(DOMAIN_TRANSFER_PAGE_URL))
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    fun onDomainTapped(domain: ProposedDomain) {
 75→        analyticsTracker.track(
 76→            AnalyticsTracker.Stat.DOMAIN_MANAGEMENT_SEARCH_DOMAIN_TAPPED,
 77→            mapOf("domain_name" to domain.domain)
 78→        )
 79→        launch {
 80→            _actionEvents.emit(
 81→                ActionEvent.PurchaseDomain(
 82→                    productId = domain.productId,
 83→                    domain = domain.domain,
 84→                    supportsPrivacy = domain.supportsPrivacy
 85→                )
 86→            )
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    fun onBackPressed() {
 91→        launch {
 92→            _actionEvents.emit(ActionEvent.GoBack)
 93→        }
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    sealed class ActionEvent {
 97→        data class PurchaseDomain(
 98→            val productId: Int,
 99→            val domain: String,
100→            val supportsPrivacy: Boolean
101→        ) : ActionEvent()
102→        data class TransferDomain(val url: String) : ActionEvent()
103→        object GoBack : ActionEvent()
104→    }
105→
106→    sealed class UiState {
107→        object Error : UiState()
108→        object Loading : UiState()
109→        data class PopulatedDomains(val domains: List<ProposedDomain>) : UiState()
110→    }
111→
112→    companion object {
113→        const val DOMAIN_TRANSFER_PAGE_URL = "https://wordpress.com/setup/domain-transfer/intro"
114→    }
115→}
116→

content.thinking:
**Examining request cancellation issues**

I'm noticing that cancellation occurs when a new emission arrives. If the old fetch coroutine has already completed, I can't prevent it from emitting the result. That means the old result might appear before the new request even starts, which seems fine because it was the "latest" at that point. However, the user might have typed a new query, and because of debounce, the new emission waits until 250ms after typing stops. This can lead to old results being displayed, which might not match the current query—definitely a concern!**Analyzing request handling logic**

I'm thinking about how results from multiple requests are supposed to load sequentially. The current setup allows only one request at a time, but maybe users see multiple because they pause between keystrokes or type quickly. They likely want any new query to cancel pending requests immediately, instead of waiting for the debounce to trigger after 250ms. As it is, continuing requests may show outdated results while typing, which could be a problem. They seem to want the UI to only update with the latest query even if the debounce hasn't fired yet.**Improving request cancellation logic**

It seems users understand that a request won't start until after the debounce period. However, if a new input comes in, they want to cancel the previous request that’s still in progress. Using collectLatest achieves this when the new debounced query is emitted. But if the user types before the debounce finishes, there’s no new request generated, which leads to stale results because the query has changed. I think it would enhance user experience to cancel any in-flight request immediately upon a query change to prevent displaying outdated results while the user types.**Clarifying request handling behavior**

The user claims that "results of multiple requests are loaded sequentially after typing," which implies that when they type a query, a request starts. If they quickly change the query, the old request might still complete and populate results before the new one does. I think canceling any search on query changes could improve the experience. Maybe I should set the loading state only on the debounced output. According to the spec, we need to ensure only the latest request updates the loading screen. The collectLatest function should help with this, as it cancels the older request when a new one starts, maintaining the loading state correctly.**Addressing query changes and request handling**

I'm considering how to handle user queries changing before new requests are started, especially with debounce involved. If a user changes their input before the debounce has triggered a new request, I could end up displaying results for an older query, which would likely confuse them. To improve this, I think canceling the ongoing request immediately upon any query change could prevent showing stale results. 

Implementing a system with a mutable state that updates on raw queries while using debounce for starting new searches sounds like a viable approach. Each new query would effectively check against ongoing requests, ensuring only the latest query informs the UI updates, meeting user expectations for real-time feedback.**Improving query handling in search functionality**

I'm working on managing the search process effectively. In the initialization phase, I should collect the debounced query and cancel any previous search job before launching a new one. This helps ensure that I’m not processing stale results. The key is implementing a job cancellation in both the 'onSearchQueryChanged' function and the initialization.

I realize I need to effectively structure this by tying job management to query changes and considering how to handle any sequential requests carefully. I think using `collect` instead of `collectLatest` might help since it allows me more control over cancellation. I also want to ensure that if the user types a query, any ongoing request should be canceled immediately to prevent outdated results from appearing in the UI.**Refining query handling with cancellations**

I'm implementing a streamlined approach to handle search queries. By using `collectLatest`, I ensure that when a user types a new query, any ongoing requests are immediately canceled, including the delay before fetching results. This method behaves like debounce without needing a separate debounce operator.

However, I notice that if the query becomes blank, I need to handle cancellations properly. Instead of filtering out blank queries, I'll check inside the `collectLatest` block, cancel the previous request, and possibly update the UI to reflect an empty state. I'll also update my imports by removing unnecessary ones. Sounds good!

content.thinking:
**Handling blank queries**

I need to update the UI state for a blank query, likely defaulting to an empty list. The initial state is `UiState.PopulatedDomains(emptyList())`, and emitting this on blank queries seems reasonable. Even if previous results were available, clearing them for an empty list avoids showing stale data, which might be better for the user experience.

However, this could be a behavioral change, so I want to consider whether emitting last results is a better alternative. Ultimately, I’ll implement `_uiStateFlow.emit(UiState.PopulatedDomains(emptyList()))` for blank queries, ensuring there's no loading stuck, focusing on avoiding potential new bugs by inspecting the expected UI behavior.

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.management.newdomainsearch

 5→import androidx.activity.viewModels
 6→import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
 7→import androidx.compose.runtime.collectAsState
 8→import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue

10→import dagger.hilt.android.AndroidEntryPoint
11→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.launchIn

13→import org.wordpress.android.ui.ActivityNavigator

15→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.management.newdomainsearch.composable.NewDomainSearchScreen
16→import javax.inject.Inject
17→
18→@AndroidEntryPoint
19→class NewDomainSearchActivity : AppCompatActivity() {
20→    private val viewModel: NewDomainSearchViewModel by viewModels()
21→
22→    @Inject
23→    lateinit var activityNavigator: ActivityNavigator
24→
25→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
26→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
27→        setContent {
28→            M3Theme {
29→                val uiState by viewModel.uiStateFlow.collectAsState()
30→                NewDomainSearchScreen(
31→                    uiState = uiState,
32→                    onSearchQueryChanged = viewModel::onSearchQueryChanged,
33→                    onTransferDomainClicked = viewModel::onTransferDomainClicked,
34→                    onDomainTapped = viewModel::onDomainTapped,
35→                    onBackPressed = viewModel::onBackPressed,
36→                )
37→            }
38→        }
39→        observeActions()
40→    }
41→
42→    private fun observeActions() {
43→        viewModel.actionEvents.onEach(this::handleActionEvents).launchIn(lifecycleScope)
44→    }
45→
46→    private fun handleActionEvents(actionEvent: NewDomainSearchViewModel.ActionEvent) {
47→        when (actionEvent) {
48→            is NewDomainSearchViewModel.ActionEvent.PurchaseDomain -> activityNavigator.openPurchaseDomain(
49→                this, actionEvent.productId, actionEvent.domain, actionEvent.supportsPrivacy
50→            )
51→
52→            is NewDomainSearchViewModel.ActionEvent.TransferDomain -> activityNavigator.openDomainTransfer(
53→                this, actionEvent.url
54→            )
55→
56→            NewDomainSearchViewModel.ActionEvent.GoBack -> onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
57→        }
58→    }
59→}

 43→@Composable
 44→fun NewDomainSearchScreen(
 45→    uiState: UiState,
 46→    onSearchQueryChanged: (String) -> Unit,
 47→    onTransferDomainClicked: () -> Unit,
 48→    onDomainTapped: (domain: ProposedDomain) -> Unit,
 49→    onBackPressed: () -> Unit,
 50→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier
 51→) {
 52→    Scaffold(
 53→        modifier = modifier,
 54→        topBar = {
 55→            MainTopAppBar(
 56→                title = stringResource(id = R.string.new_domain_search_screen_title),
 57→                navigationIcon = NavigationIcons.BackIcon,
 58→                onNavigationIconClick = onBackPressed,
 59→                backgroundColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surface,
 60→                contentColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface,
 61→            )
 62→        },
 63→        content = {
 64→            Column(
 65→                modifier = Modifier.padding(it),
 66→            ) {
 67→                val listState = rememberLazyListState()
 68→
 69→                val elevation = animateDpAsState(
 70→                    targetValue = if (listState.canScrollBackward) 4.dp else 0.dp,
 71→                    label = "Search Input Elevation",
 72→                )
 73→                NewDomainSearchInput(elevation = elevation.value, onValueChanged = onSearchQueryChanged)
 74→                when (uiState) {
 75→                    is UiState.PopulatedDomains -> ProposedDomainList(
 76→                        domains = uiState.domains,
 77→                        listState = listState,
 78→                        onDomainTapped = onDomainTapped,
 79→                        modifier = Modifier.weight(1f)
 80→                    )
 81→                    is UiState.Loading -> LoadingPlaceholder(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f))
 82→                    is UiState.Error -> Spacer(modifier = Modifier.weight(1f))
 83→                }
 84→                TransferDomainFooter(onTransferDomainClicked = onTransferDomainClicked)
 85→            }
 86→        }
 87→    )
 88→}
 89→

 92→    elevation: Dp,
 93→    onValueChanged: (String) -> Unit,
 94→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier
 95→) {
 96→    var queryString by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf("") }
 97→
 98→    Surface(shadowElevation = elevation, modifier = Modifier.zIndex(1f)) {
 99→        DomainsSearchTextField(
100→            value = queryString,
101→            enabled = true,
102→            placeholder = R.string.new_domain_search_screen_input_placeholder,
103→            modifier = modifier,
104→            onValueChange = {
105→                queryString = it
106→                onValueChanged(it)
107→            }
108→        )
109→    }
110→}
111→
112→@Composable
113→fun ProposedDomainList(
114→    domains: List<ProposedDomain>,
115→    listState: LazyListState,
116→    onDomainTapped: (domain: ProposedDomain) -> Unit,
117→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier
118→) {
119→    LazyColumn(
120→        state = listState,
121→        modifier = modifier.fillMaxWidth()
122→    ) {
123→        items(items = domains) { domain -> Domain(domain = domain, onDomainTapped = onDomainTapped) }
124→    }
125→}
126→
127→@Composable
128→fun LoadingPlaceholder(modifier: Modifier = Modifier) {
129→    Column(modifier = modifier) {
130→        repeat(times = 2) {
131→            Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(16.dp)) {
132→                PendingGhostStrip(width = 100.dp)
133→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(4.dp))
134→                Row {
135→                    PendingGhostStrip(width = 150.dp)
136→                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(4.dp))
137→                    PendingGhostStrip(width = 80.dp)
138→                }
139→            }
140→        }
141→    }

content.thinking:
**Ensuring correct cancellation behavior**

I’m ensuring that there are no unused imports and everything lints well, which is great. I want to confirm if cancellation is working. With `collectLatest`, it should cancel `delay` and `fetchDomains` if the query changes. If a new query comes in while the first is loading, it cancels the coroutine to avoid emitting an incorrect final state. The new query will either emit an empty list or run a delay, making it look like a loading screen, which is acceptable.**Verifying cancellation behavior**

When the query changes and a previous fetch is still running, it cancels that request to start a new block, which includes a delay. During this delay, the Loading state isn't set unless it was already set by a previous request. If it's the user's first input, a non-blank query triggers the delay and then fetchDomains, which does set Loading after 250ms. If the user clears the query, we should immediately show an empty list. Now, I need to ensure that no other flows are calling fetchDomains concurrently, and searchForDomains must be cancellable.**Ensuring fresh UI updates**

To avoid the UI being updated with stale results, even if the repository call isn't cancellable, I could implement a generation check, although it might seem excessive. If the repository uses OkHttp synchronously, that could be tricky, but it likely uses coroutines with Retrofit, making it cancellable. To be extra safe, I’ll consider implementing a "latest request ID" check to ensure results reflect only the latest query. I can maintain `private val searchSequence = AtomicLong(0)` and `private var searchRequestId = 0` to handle this efficiently.**Implementing request ID checks**

When the query changes, there's a chance that an in-flight request might still update the UI state due to ID mismatches. Kotlin coroutines should handle cancellations well, but tests could potentially simulate a situation where the repository returns data without cancellation. To ensure that only the response to the latest request updates the UI state, implementing a robust request ID check seems wise.

I’ll create a `private var latestSearchRequestId = 0L` and increment it in `collectLatest` when the query is non-blank. This ID will be passed to `fetchDomains` to manage loading states effectively.**Implementing request ID checks in fetchDomains**

In `collectLatest`, I’ll increment `latestSearchRequestId` for each query. During the delay, I'll check if the current `requestId` matches `latestSearchRequestId`, but if the query changes, `collectLatest` cancels this, making the check unnecessary. In the `fetchDomains` function, I'll emit Loading, perform the repository call, and only emit final results if the request ID matches.

It's crucial that `latestSearchRequestId` increments even for blank queries to invalidate previous requests. To handle potential concurrency issues, I’ll use `@Volatile` for `searchRequestId` and consider using `AtomicLong` for safety. This seems like a solid plan!

Alright, I need to finalize the compilation. It looks like the fetchDomains signature has been changed and is only called during initialization, which is good. I’ll check if onSearchQueryChanged is using launch with trimming. I also want to ensure that requestId increments even on blank inputs to cancel previous ones. That sounds like it should work. content.text:
Implemented stale-request cancellation on the New Domain Search screen.

### What was happening
`fetchDomains()` was being executed sequentially; older in-flight requests could still finish and update `uiStateFlow`, causing the UI to briefly show results/loading states from outdated queries.

### Fix
Updated `NewDomainSearchViewModel` to:
- Use `collectLatest` so any new query immediately cancels the previous in-flight work (including the debounce wait and the repository call).
- Keep the existing debounce behavior via a cancellable `delay`.
- Guard UI updates with a monotonically increasing `latestSearchRequestId`, so even if a request can’t be cancelled promptly, its result won’t update the UI unless it’s still the latest.
- Reset UI to an empty list when the query becomes blank (and invalidate any in-flight request).

Changed file:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/domains/management/newdomainsearch/NewDomainSearchViewModel.kt`

 15→import org.wordpress.android.analytics.AnalyticsTracker
 16→import org.wordpress.android.modules.UI_THREAD
 17→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.management.newdomainsearch.domainsfetcher.NewDomainsSearchRepository
 18→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.management.newdomainsearch.domainsfetcher.ProposedDomain
 19→import org.wordpress.android.util.analytics.AnalyticsTrackerWrapper
 20→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ScopedViewModel
 21→import javax.inject.Inject
 22→import javax.inject.Named
 23→
 24→private const val SEARCH_QUERY_DELAY_MS = 250L
 25→
 26→@OptIn(FlowPreview::class)
 27→@HiltViewModel
 28→class NewDomainSearchViewModel @Inject constructor(
 29→    @Named(UI_THREAD) private val mainDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 30→    private val newDomainsSearchRepository: NewDomainsSearchRepository,
 31→    private val analyticsTracker: AnalyticsTrackerWrapper
 32→) : ScopedViewModel(mainDispatcher) {
 33→    private val _actionEvents = MutableSharedFlow<ActionEvent>()
 34→    val actionEvents: Flow<ActionEvent> = _actionEvents
 35→
 36→    private val _uiStateFlow: MutableStateFlow<UiState> = MutableStateFlow(UiState.PopulatedDomains(emptyList()))
 37→    val uiStateFlow = _uiStateFlow.asStateFlow()
 38→
 39→    private val debouncedQuery = MutableStateFlow("")
 40→
 41→    init {
 42→        analyticsTracker.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.DOMAIN_MANAGEMENT_SEARCH_FOR_A_DOMAIN_SCREEN_SHOWN)
 43→        debouncedQuery
 44→            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
 45→            .debounce(SEARCH_QUERY_DELAY_MS)
 46→            .onEach(::fetchDomains)
 47→            .launchIn(viewModelScope)
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    private suspend fun fetchDomains(query: String) {
 51→        _uiStateFlow.emit(UiState.Loading)
 52→        val result = newDomainsSearchRepository.searchForDomains(query)
 53→        _uiStateFlow.emit(
 54→            when (result) {
 55→                is NewDomainsSearchRepository.DomainsResult.Success -> UiState.PopulatedDomains(result.proposedDomains)
 56→                is NewDomainsSearchRepository.DomainsResult.Error -> UiState.Error
 57→            }
 58→        )
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    fun onSearchQueryChanged(query: String) {
 62→        launch {
 63→           debouncedQuery.emit(query.trim())
 64→        }
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    fun onTransferDomainClicked() {
 68→        analyticsTracker.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.DOMAIN_MANAGEMENT_TRANSFER_DOMAIN_TAPPED)
 69→        launch {
 70→            _actionEvents.emit(ActionEvent.TransferDomain(DOMAIN_TRANSFER_PAGE_URL))
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    fun onDomainTapped(domain: ProposedDomain) {
 75→        analyticsTracker.track(
 76→            AnalyticsTracker.Stat.DOMAIN_MANAGEMENT_SEARCH_DOMAIN_TAPPED,
 77→            mapOf("domain_name" to domain.domain)
 78→        )
 79→        launch {
 80→            _actionEvents.emit(
 81→                ActionEvent.PurchaseDomain(
 82→                    productId = domain.productId,
 83→                    domain = domain.domain,
 84→                    supportsPrivacy = domain.supportsPrivacy
 85→                )
 86→            )
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    fun onBackPressed() {
 91→        launch {
 92→            _actionEvents.emit(ActionEvent.GoBack)
 93→        }
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    sealed class ActionEvent {
 97→        data class PurchaseDomain(
 98→            val productId: Int,
 99→            val domain: String,
100→            val supportsPrivacy: Boolean
101→        ) : ActionEvent()
102→        data class TransferDomain(val url: String) : ActionEvent()
103→        object GoBack : ActionEvent()
104→    }
105→
106→    sealed class UiState {
107→        object Error : UiState()
108→        object Loading : UiState()
109→        data class PopulatedDomains(val domains: List<ProposedDomain>) : UiState()
110→    }
111→
112→    companion object {
113→        const val DOMAIN_TRANSFER_PAGE_URL = "https://wordpress.com/setup/domain-transfer/intro"
114→    }
115→}

 85→            }

115→    listState: LazyListState,
116→    onDomainTapped: (domain: ProposedDomain) -> Unit,
117→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier
118→) {
119→    LazyColumn(
120→        state = listState,
121→        modifier = modifier.fillMaxWidth()
122→    ) {
123→        items(items = domains) { domain -> Domain(domain = domain, onDomainTapped = onDomainTapped) }
124→    }
125→}
126→
127→@Composable
128→fun LoadingPlaceholder(modifier: Modifier = Modifier) {
129→    Column(modifier = modifier) {
130→        repeat(times = 2) {
131→            Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(16.dp)) {
132→                PendingGhostStrip(width = 100.dp)
133→                Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(4.dp))
134→                Row {
135→                    PendingGhostStrip(width = 150.dp)
136→                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(4.dp))
137→                    PendingGhostStrip(width = 80.dp)
138→                }
139→            }
140→        }
141→    }
142→}
143→
144→@Composable
145→fun TransferDomainFooter(
146→    onTransferDomainClicked: () -> Unit,
147→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier
148→) {
149→    Surface(shadowElevation = 8.dp, modifier = Modifier.zIndex(1f)) {
150→        Column(
151→            modifier = modifier
152→                .padding(16.dp)
153→        ) {
154→            Text(
155→                text = stringResource(id = R.string.new_domain_search_screen_transfer_domain_description),
156→                style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
157→            )
158→            JetpackOutlinedButton(
159→                text = stringResource(R.string.new_domain_search_screen_transfer_domain_button),
160→                onClick = onTransferDomainClicked
161→            )
162→        }
163→    }
164→}
165→
166→@Composable
167→fun Domain(
168→    domain: ProposedDomain,
169→    onDomainTapped: (domain: ProposedDomain) -> Unit,
170→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier
171→) {
172→    Surface(
173→        onClick = { onDomainTapped(domain) },
174→        modifier = modifier.fillMaxWidth()
175→    ) {
176→        Column(modifier = Modifier.padding(16.dp)) {
177→            Row {
178→                Text(
179→                    text = domain.domainPrefix,
180→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge
181→                )
182→                Text(
183→                    text = domain.domainSuffix,
184→                    style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge,
185→                    fontWeight = FontWeight.Bold
186→                )
187→            }
188→            if (domain.salePrice.isNullOrEmpty()) {
189→                Text(
190→                    text = stringResource(
191→                        id = R.string.new_domain_search_screen_list_item_regular_price,
192→                        domain.price
193→                    )
194→                )
195→            } else {
196→                Row {
197→                    Text(
198→                        text = stringResource(
199→                            id = R.string.new_domain_search_screen_list_item_sale_price, domain.salePrice
200→                        ),
201→                        modifier = Modifier.padding(end = 4.dp),
202→                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
203→                        fontWeight = FontWeight.Medium,
204→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.success
205→                    )
206→                    Text(
207→                        text = stringResource(
208→                            id = R.string.new_domain_search_screen_list_item_regular_price,
209→                            domain.price
210→                        ),
211→                        textDecoration = TextDecoration.LineThrough,
212→                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface.copy(alpha = 0.6f)
213→                    )
214→                }
215→            }
216→        }
217→    }
218→}
